#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Crop a region from an image. No resizing, no interpolation — original pixels only."""
import sys
from PIL import Image

if len(sys.argv) != 7:
    print("Usage: python3 crop.py input.png x1 y1 x2 y2 output.png")
    print("  Crops the rectangle (x1,y1)-(x2,y2) from input and saves to output.")
    print("  Coordinates are in pixels from top-left corner.")
    sys.exit(1)

input_path, x1, y1, x2, y2, output_path = sys.argv[1], int(sys.argv[2]), int(sys.argv[3]), int(sys.argv[4]), int(sys.argv[5]), sys.argv[6]
img = Image.open(input_path)
w, h = img.size
x1, y1, x2, y2 = max(0, x1), max(0, y1), min(w, x2), min(h, y2)
crop = img.crop((x1, y1, x2, y2))
crop.save(output_path)
print(f"Cropped {input_path} ({w}x{h}) -> {output_path} ({crop.size[0]}x{crop.size[1]})")

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Analyze the Simpsons heatmap image to find the 2nd lowest rated episode."""

from PIL import Image
import numpy as np

IMG_PATH = "/Users/mugen/Codebase/chart_derendering/data/chartmuseum/images/simpsons-ratings.png"

img = Image.open(IMG_PATH)
print(f"Image size: {img.size}")
print(f"Image mode: {img.mode}")

arr = np.array(img.convert("RGB"))
print(f"Array shape: {arr.shape}")

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Identify the color scale
#    The colorbar shows: teal/green ≈ 9.2 (high), orange/brown ≈ 4.5 (low)
#    "Brownest" / lowest-rated cells will have high R, low G, low B values
#    (typical brown: R~150-200, G~70-100, B~40-70)
#    Teal/cyan high-rated: R~50-100, G~160-190, B~170-200
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Sample a known extreme-low pixel: the annotated Lady Gaga episode is visible
# in the lower-right area of the grid (bottom ~third, right area).
# Let's first look at the image structure.

# Print some pixels from across the image to understand coordinates
h, w = arr.shape[:2]
print(f"\nImage height={h}, width={w}")

# Sample the colorbar area to understand the color mapping
# The colorbar appears to be in the top-right area of the image (~row 30-70, col 400-550)
print("\n--- Colorbar region samples ---")
for row in [35, 40, 45, 50]:
    for col in range(390, 560, 10):
        r, g, b = arr[row, col]
        print(f"  ({col},{row}): RGB({r},{g},{b})")

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Find the heatmap grid boundaries
#    The heatmap has rows = seasons (1-27) and columns = episodes (variable per season)
#    From the image, the grid starts around y=60 and ends near y=730
#    and x starts around 60 and ends around 530
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Let's scan vertically and horizontally to find the grid edges
# by looking for transitions from white/grey background to colored cells

# Sample a column in the middle of the grid area to find row boundaries
mid_col = 200
print(f"\n--- Vertical scan at x={mid_col} (looking for grid rows) ---")
prev_is_bg = True
row_starts = []
for row in range(50, h - 10):
    r, g, b = arr[row, mid_col]
    # Background pixels tend to be white (>240) or very light
    is_bg = (r > 230 and g > 230 and b > 230)
    if prev_is_bg and not is_bg:
        row_starts.append(row)
        print(f"  Row start at y={row}: RGB({r},{g},{b})")
    elif not prev_is_bg and is_bg:
        print(f"  Row end   at y={row}: RGB({r},{g},{b})")
    prev_is_bg = is_bg

# Sample a row in the middle of the grid area to find column boundaries
mid_row = 150
print(f"\n--- Horizontal scan at y={mid_row} (looking for grid cols) ---")
prev_is_bg = True
col_starts = []
for col in range(30, w - 10):
    r, g, b = arr[mid_row, col]
    is_bg = (r > 230 and g > 230 and b > 230)
    if prev_is_bg and not is_bg:
        col_starts.append(col)
        print(f"  Col start at x={col}: RGB({r},{g},{b})")
    elif not prev_is_bg and is_bg:
        print(f"  Col end   at x={col}: RGB({r},{g},{b})")
    prev_is_bg = is_bg

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Analyze the Simpsons heatmap image to find the 2nd lowest rated episode."""

from PIL import Image
import numpy as np

IMG_PATH = "/Users/mugen/Codebase/chart_derendering/data/chartmuseum/images/simpsons-ratings.png"

img = Image.open(IMG_PATH)
print(f"Image size: {img.size}")
print(f"Image mode: {img.mode}")

arr = np.array(img.convert("RGB"))
print(f"Array shape: {arr.shape}")

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Identify the color scale
#    The colorbar shows: teal/green ≈ 9.2 (high), orange/brown ≈ 4.5 (low)
#    "Brownest" / lowest-rated cells will have high R, low G, low B values
#    (typical brown: R~150-200, G~70-100, B~40-70)
#    Teal/cyan high-rated: R~50-100, G~160-190, B~170-200
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Sample a known extreme-low pixel: the annotated Lady Gaga episode is visible
# in the lower-right area of the grid (bottom ~third, right area).
# Let's first look at the image structure.

# Print some pixels from across the image to understand coordinates
h, w = arr.shape[:2]
print(f"\nImage height={h}, width={w}")

# Sample the colorbar area to understand the color mapping
# The colorbar appears to be in the top-right area of the image (~row 30-70, col 400-550)
print("\n--- Colorbar region samples ---")
for row in [35, 40, 45, 50]:
    for col in range(390, 560, 10):
        r, g, b = arr[row, col]
        print(f"  ({col},{row}): RGB({r},{g},{b})")

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Find the heatmap grid boundaries
#    The heatmap has rows = seasons (1-27) and columns = episodes (variable per season)
#    From the image, the grid starts around y=60 and ends near y=730
#    and x starts around 60 and ends around 530
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Let's scan vertically and horizontally to find the grid edges
# by looking for transitions from white/grey background to colored cells

# Sample a column in the middle of the grid area to find row boundaries
mid_col = 200
print(f"\n--- Vertical scan at x={mid_col} (looking for grid rows) ---")
prev_is_bg = True
row_starts = []
for row in range(50, h - 10):
    r, g, b = arr[row, mid_col]
    # Background pixels tend to be white (>240) or very light
    is_bg = (r > 230 and g > 230 and b > 230)
    if prev_is_bg and not is_bg:
        row_starts.append(row)
        print(f"  Row start at y={row}: RGB({r},{g},{b})")
    elif not prev_is_bg and is_bg:
        print(f"  Row end   at y={row}: RGB({r},{g},{b})")
    prev_is_bg = is_bg

# Sample a row in the middle of the grid area to find column boundaries
mid_row = 150
print(f"\n--- Horizontal scan at y={mid_row} (looking for grid cols) ---")
prev_is_bg = True
col_starts = []
for col in range(30, w - 10):
    r, g, b = arr[mid_row, col]
    is_bg = (r > 230 and g > 230 and b > 230)
    if prev_is_bg and not is_bg:
        col_starts.append(col)
        print(f"  Col start at x={col}: RGB({r},{g},{b})")
    elif not prev_is_bg and is_bg:
        print(f"  Col end   at x={col}: RGB({r},{g},{b})")
    prev_is_bg = is_bg
